fixedSinceLastReview: hover card now uses STATUS_LABELS and STATUS_EMOJIS (was raw lowercase)
vibeStreak prop correctly passed to Office component
workers.json roster key present — worker filter bar functional
stuck detection uses worker.startedAt instead of .started

suggestions.title: ShortcutToast timer leak — timerRef overwritten on 'Got it' dismiss path
suggestions.description: ShortcutToast dismiss() (App.jsx ~line 530): when gotIt=true, timerRef.current is assigned a 500ms timeout (setPhase 'fading-out'), then IMMEDIATELY overwritten with a 900ms timeout (setPhase 'hidden'). The cleanup useEffect only clears timerRef.current — so the 500ms timer is orphaned and fires after unmount, calling setState on a potentially dead component. Fix: use two refs or chain with a single setTimeout.

suggestions.title: ProgressBar ETA uses updated_at (last poll time) instead of startedAt
suggestions.description: ProgressBar component (App.jsx ~line 193): `workingMs = Date.now() - new Date(updatedAt).getTime()` — but updatedAt is worker.updated_at which advances every 30s sync. A worker at 10% who started 40 minutes ago but was last synced 30s ago shows ETA of ~4 minutes instead of ~360 minutes. The workers.json already has startedAt which is set once. Fix: pass worker.startedAt to ProgressBar and use it for ETA calculation.

suggestions.title: React.memo on WorkerCard defeated — activityLog is a new array reference every 30s
suggestions.description: syncFromGitHub (App.jsx ~line 691): `setActivityLog(Array.isArray(activity) ? activity : [])` creates a new array ref each poll. This array is passed as activityEntries to every WorkerCard. Since React.memo uses shallow comparison, ALL cards re-render every 30s regardless of whether their data changed. The memo is completely defeated. Fix: useMemo to create per-worker activity slices, or use a stable ref for the full log and only derive worker-specific entries when needed.

suggestions.title: VibeSparkline reads localStorage BEFORE parent writes — always one entry behind
suggestions.description: React runs child effects before parent effects. StatsBar's useEffect reads vibeHistory from localStorage BEFORE App.jsx's useEffect writes the new vibe entry. The sparkline permanently lags by one vibe change. Fix: lift vibeHistory state into App.jsx, compute it in the same effect that writes to localStorage, and pass the array as a prop to StatsBar.

suggestions.title: Office floor characters unreadably small at 375px — .char__name is ~7px
suggestions.description: On mobile at 375px width, the office floor renders at about 330x248px. The .char__name labels at 0.44rem are barely 7px — illegible on any phone. The whiteboard text overlaps the leftmost desk column. Idle workers wander into the conference table area due to percentage-based positioning. Fix: switch office floor to aspect-ratio: 3/4 on mobile, increase min-height to 480px, and bump .char__name to 0.6rem.

suggestions.title: getRelativeTime duplicated verbatim in Office.jsx ghost bubble
suggestions.description: App.jsx exports getRelativeTime(timestamp) as a utility. Office.jsx Character component (~line 432) has an identical inline IIFE doing the exact same mins/hours/days math for the ghost bubble 'Last seen' display — it's not imported. Two places to maintain. Fix: import getRelativeTime from App.jsx or extract to utils/time.js.

suggestions.title: Office floor character details are hover-only — keyboard and touch users locked out
suggestions.description: Character hover cards (Office.jsx ~line 473) only appear on mouseenter/mouseleave for working/idle variants. Touch users and keyboard users get no worker details when interacting with a character. The tabIndex={0} implies keyboard support but there's no focus handler showing the hover card for non-manager characters. Fix: the onFocus/onBlur handlers are now wired but the hover card only shows for working/idle variants on mouse events — verify the focus path also triggers setHovered(true).

suggestions.title: completionToast rendered with 12+ inline styles — unthemeable and unmaintainable
suggestions.description: App.jsx ~line 942: the completion toast div has a massive inline style object (position, bottom, left, transform, background, border, borderRadius, padding, fontSize, fontWeight, color, boxShadow, zIndex, whiteSpace, pointerEvents, animation). Only border-color is dynamic. Everything else is static and should be a CSS class. It also doesn't follow the light theme properly since colors are hardcoded. Fix: extract to .completion-toast class in App.css.

suggestions.title: StatsBar scroll-to-stuck-worker uses document.querySelector — breaks on Office tab
suggestions.description: StatsBar (~line 451): `document.querySelector('[data-worker-name="${name}"]')?.scrollIntoView(...)` does imperative DOM access. This only works when Dashboard tab is active (cards are mounted). If user is on Office tab and clicks the stuck worker stat card, nothing happens. Fix: use the existing focusedWorker state + handleWorkerClick pattern to switch to dashboard tab first.

suggestions.title: Fading worker cards all get index={activeWorkers.length} — stagger animation broken
suggestions.description: App.jsx ~line 1102: all fading cards share the same CSS --i value. When 2+ workers depart simultaneously, they all vanish in sync instead of cascading. Fix: use an incrementing offset or the worker's original index.

suggestions.title: 19 ambient particle DOM nodes with permanent CSS animations — jank on mobile
suggestions.description: App.jsx renders 12 ambient particles and Office.jsx renders 7 more. All run permanent CSS animations. On mid-range mobile hardware this causes measurable jank during the 30s sync cycle. prefers-reduced-motion disables them, but only for users who set that preference. Fix: reduce count to 6+4 on mobile via media query, or use a single CSS background animation.
